about.title: About MagellAIn
about.subtitle: Sailing Intelligence for Great Lakes Racers
about.description: MagellAIn is a sailing intelligence platform that combines real-time weather and water data, AI-powered coaching, race management and analytics, GPS track recording and replay, race course tactical analysis, start line bias calculation, wind shift tracking, layline overlay, pre-race checklist, AI-powered route planning, offline support, and safety features into a single app designed for racing sailors on Lake Erie and the Detroit River.
about.builtFor: Ford Yacht Club, West Shore Sail Club, and the DRYA racing community

features.title: Live Marine Data
features.summary: Real-time NOAA weather, NDBC buoy observations, and marine forecasts displayed on interactive charts.
features.details: Wind speed and direction, wave height, water temperature, and marine forecasts updated continuously from official government sources. Year-round shore stations include Toledo Harbor (THRO1), South Bass Island OH (SBIO1), and Marblehead OH (MRHO1). Seasonal offshore buoys — Toledo Channel Light (45132), W Lake Erie Buoy (45005), and Cleveland Buoy (45142) — deploy in late spring. Active NOAA weather alerts (Small Craft Advisories, Gale Warnings) appear as prominent banners on the Home and Weather screens.

features.title: AI Sailing Coach
features.summary: A Claude-powered assistant that streams real-time responses with live weather context, voice input, and full knowledge of your boat and local waters.
features.details: Ask about tactics, sail trim, weather interpretation, racing rules, or post-race analysis. The coach receives live NDBC buoy readings injected into its context at the time of every message — so when you ask what the wind is doing, it references actual observed conditions, not a general forecast. Responses stream token-by-token so you start reading within seconds. Tap the microphone button to ask hands-free using voice input.

features.title: Pre-Race Start Timer
features.summary: The classic 5-minute US Sailing gun sequence with audio signals, built for one-handed cockpit use.
features.details: Open the Timer tab in the bottom tab bar on the map and tap '5 MIN' to start the Warning → Prep → One Minute → Start sequence. Audio beeps sound at each transition with a long horn tone at the gun. Tap 'SYNC' to lock the timer to an actual race committee gun. Large countdown display with color-coded phase labels — yellow for Warning, orange for Prep, red for One Minute, green for Racing.

features.title: Wind Arrow Overlay
features.summary: Live observed wind vectors drawn at every active NDBC station across Western Lake Erie and the Detroit River.
features.details: Toggle on from the map controls panel. Directional arrows appear at each monitoring station showing where the wind is blowing toward. Color scales from green (light air) through yellow, orange, and red as wind speed increases. Size also scales with speed. Tap any arrow for a station popup showing speed in knots, compass direction, degrees, and gusts.

features.title: Race Courses and Tactical Analysis
features.summary: Select any FYC or WSSC course and get leg-by-leg tactical advice using live wind data.
features.details: The Race Courses panel on the Map screen lets you choose from all active race courses stored in the database. Selecting a course shows its legs: mark name, rounding direction, bearing, and distance. Toggle course marks on the chart as colored pins. Tap 'AI Tactical Analysis' to see each leg's point of sail (Close Hauled, Beam Reach, Running, etc.) calculated from current NDBC wind direction, with specific tactical advice for conditions — upwind depower tips in heavy air, VMG running guidance downwind, spinnaker advice on reaching legs.

features.title: Start Line Bias Tool
features.summary: Mark the two ends of your start line on the chart and instantly see which end is favored based on live wind data.
features.details: Open the Start Line Bias tool from the Tools tab in the bottom drawer on the map. Tap 'Boat / Pin End' then tap its position on the chart, then do the same for 'Committee.' MagellAIn calculates the bias angle — how far the line deviates from perpendicular to the wind — and tells you which end is favored and by how many degrees. The line draws on the chart in amber with color-coded pins. Bias under 2 degrees is flagged as a square line. Wind direction is pulled automatically from the nearest active NDBC buoy. Use in the 5-15 minutes before your start gun.

features.title: Wind Shift Tracking
features.summary: See the last 90 minutes of wind direction history from any active NDBC station as a live chart on the map or weather screen.
features.details: The Wind Shift panel on the Map screen (accessible via the Tools tab in the bottom drawer) and the Wind Shift History section on the Weather page (near the top of the weather screen) show a time-series chart of wind direction over the last 90 minutes from any NDBC shore station you select. The chart highlights veering (clockwise shift) or backing (counter-clockwise shift) trends and shows the current wind speed. Veering wind in the northern hemisphere typically signals an approaching warm front; backing may indicate building pressure. Data is fetched from the NDBC realtime observation feed, typically updated every 10-30 minutes.

features.title: Layline Overlay
features.summary: See port and starboard laylines to the windward mark drawn directly on the chart, with an adjustable tacking angle.
features.details: Enable laylines from the Layers panel in map controls. Green dashes show the port tack layline, red dashes show the starboard tack layline — both extending from the windward mark toward the fleet. The tacking angle slider (30-55 degrees, default 42) lets you tune the calculation for your boat class and conditions. Laylines update automatically as wind direction changes from NDBC observations. A course must be selected in the Course Panel for laylines to draw.

features.title: Pre-Race Checklist
features.summary: A comprehensive 30-item race preparation checklist covering rig, sails, safety, boat systems, and race day specifics.
features.details: The Pre-Race Checklist lives on the Races page (Menu > Races) as a collapsible card at the top of the page, always accessible alongside your race schedule. Five accordion sections — Rig, Sails, Safety, Boat, and Race Day — contain the key items every racing sailor should verify before the start gun. A live progress bar tracks completion. Items can be checked off individually; Reset clears all checks for the next race. The checklist is session-based and resets when the app reloads. Designed for one-handed use on the water.

features.title: GPS Track Recording
features.summary: Record your track on the water with live speed, distance, and time stats.
features.details: The GPS Tracker panel on the Map screen lets you start recording your position, speed (in knots), and heading using your device GPS. Live stats show elapsed time, current speed, total nautical miles, and max speed. Tracks save to your account when signed in, or to local device storage for offline use.

features.title: Track Replay
features.summary: Replay any saved GPS track on the map with animated playback and variable speed.
features.details: Open the Track Replay panel from the Route icon on the map to see all your saved sessions. Select any track to draw the full route in orange on the chart. Play, Pause, or Stop the animated dot as it moves through your recorded positions. Choose 1x, 5x, 20x, or 50x playback speed to review a two-hour race in seconds. Track replay is available for both Supabase-synced and locally stored tracks.

features.title: Race Management and Analytics
features.summary: Club schedules, results, series standings, CSV result import, head-to-head records, and performance trend charts.
features.details: View upcoming and past races from Ford YC, West Shore SC, and DRYA series. Import race results from any CSV export (YachtScoring, PHRF-LE, or spreadsheet) using the four-step wizard: upload, column mapping, race selection, and preview. The Performance screen (Menu > Performance) shows 19 metrics per boat: race count, wins, podiums, average position, season trend chart, series standings per regatta, full race log, and head-to-head win/loss records against same-fleet competitors with PHRF correction details. Export your race log and standings as a PDF.

features.title: AI Route Planner
features.summary: AI-powered passage planning with weather routing and boat polar performance optimization.
features.details: Select from pre-loaded Great Lakes passages (Lake St. Clair Circuit, Ford YC to Lake Erie, Detroit River to Put-in-Bay, Port Huron to Mackinac) or create custom routes. The planner computes optimal routes factoring in weather forecasts, your boat's polar performance data, and PHRF rating. Results show total distance, estimated duration, recommended sails per leg, and a comparison to the straight rhumb-line route. Accessible from the Route tab in the bottom navigation.

features.title: Offline Support
features.summary: The app stays functional when you lose signal — cached pages, offline GPS tracking, and an offline indicator.
features.details: A service worker caches all main screens and recently loaded data so you can keep using MagellAIn underway without a connection. GPS track recording works fully offline, saving to local storage until sync is available. An amber banner appears at the top of the screen when offline and clears automatically when you reconnect. NOAA weather and buoy data remains available from cache for up to one hour after the last fetch.

features.title: Safety First
features.summary: Emergency USCG contact, SOS text with GPS coordinates, float plans, and weather alert banners.
features.details: One-tap calling to 911, USCG Sector Detroit (313-568-9564), and USCG Station Toledo (419-691-4048). Get your GPS coordinates displayed for relay to rescue services. Send an SOS text with your Google Maps location link to any saved emergency contact. File digital float plans pre-filled with your vessel details before heading out. Active NOAA weather alerts appear as prominent banners on the Home and Weather screens whenever conditions warrant caution.

features.title: Live Sailing News
features.summary: A curated sailing news feed on the home screen pulling from top publications.
features.details: The home screen includes a live sailing news feed with recent articles from Sail Magazine, Scuttlebutt Sailing News, World Sailing, and Sailing World. Articles load automatically and show title, excerpt, source, image, and time since publication. Tap any article to open it in your browser.

features.title: Built for the Water
features.summary: Clean, one-handed mobile interface that works when conditions are tough.
features.details: Designed for use on a wet, moving boat with one hand. Large touch targets, high-contrast dark and light display themes, and voice input when your hands are full. No clutter, no distractions — a professional tool for serious sailors.

dataSources.name: NOAA / NWS
dataSources.description: Marine weather forecasts, zone alerts (Small Craft Advisory, Gale Warning), and National Weather Service gridpoint data for Lake Erie

dataSources.name: NDBC Buoys
dataSources.description: Real-time wind, wave, water temperature, and pressure observations from active Great Lakes monitoring stations

dataSources.name: NOAA CO-OPS
dataSources.description: Water levels and tidal current data for the Detroit River

dataSources.name: YachtScoring / PHRF-LE
dataSources.description: Race results and regatta data importable via CSV export from local clubs

dataSources.name: Supabase
dataSources.description: Cloud database for race courses, marks, regatta results, GPS tracks, boat profiles, and user data

legal.disclaimer: MagellAIn is a decision-support tool, not a substitute for seamanship, proper safety equipment, or sound judgment. Weather data is sourced from NOAA and may be delayed. Always monitor VHF Channel 16 and carry required safety gear. The emergency feature connects to USCG but does not replace a VHF radio or EPIRB.
legal.dataAttribution: Weather and marine data provided by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Weather Service (NWS), and National Data Buoy Center (NDBC).
